title: lepidium campestre subsp. hirtum
intro: Lepidium campestre subsp. hirtum, commonly known as hairy field cress, is a subspecies of the field cress plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of hairy field cress include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Lepidium campestre subsp. hirtum can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.
